About: It is about a quest to destroy the One Ring.
Affects: It affects the modern fantasy genre.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with J. R. R. Tolkien.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the high fantasy genre.
CreatedBy: It was created by J. R. R. Tolkien.
DevelopedBy: It was developed by J. R. R. Tolkien.
HasA: It has numerous references in popular culture.
It has inspired many derivative works.
It has been reprinted many times.
It has been translated into at least 38 languages.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by its themes, literary devices, and origins.
It is characterized by its enduring popularity.
Influenced: It influenced the modern fantasy genre.
It influenced paintings, music, films, television, video games, and board games.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by earlier fantasy works.
It was influenced by Tolkien's experiences in the First World War.
IsA: It is an epic high fantasy novel.
It is one of the best-selling books ever written.
It is considered one of the most influential fantasy books ever written.
It is often called a trilogy.
It is divided internally into six books.
It is named Britain's best-loved novel of all time in a 2003 poll by the BBC called The Big Read.
KnownAs: It is known as a sequel to The Hobbit.
It is known as a single volume in a two-volume set along with The Silmarillion.
KnownFor: It is known for its quest to destroy the One Ring.
It is known for its characters Frodo, Sam, Merry, and Pippin.
It is known for its characters Gandalf, Aragorn, Boromir, Legolas, and Gimli.
It is known for its antagonist the Dark Lord Sauron.
It is known for its campaign to conquer Middle-earth.
OccurredIn: It occurred in Middle-earth.
It occurred in the Shire.
It occurred in Rivendell.
It occurred in the Mines of Moria.
It occurred in Lothlórien.
It occurred in Fangorn Forest.
It occurred in Edoras.
It occurred in Helm's Deep.
It occurred in Isengard.
It occurred in Mordor.
PartOf: It is part of a two-volume set with The Silmarillion.
PublishedAs: It was published as a boxed set in 1957.
It was published as a single volume in 1968.
PublishedIn: It was published over the course of a year from 29 July 1954 to 20 October 1955.
WrittenBy: It was written by J. R. R. Tolkien.
WrittenIn: It was written in stages between 1937 and 1949.
